commands.review_summary:
The PR adds optional per-turn `usageState` metadata and provider usage-limit windows to `reply_payload_sending`, recorded after each agent turn and consumed during reply dispatch.

PR surface: Source +309. Total +309 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows routed replies queue and replay `replyPayloadSendingHook` without `usageState`, and the usage resolver defaults OpenAI limits to OAuth unless the active credential type is supplied.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor real behavior proof and maintainer review of the plugin API/auth-provider contract rather than an automated repair marker.

target.title: feat(hooks): per-turn usageState (with provider usage limits) on reply_payload_sending
target.body:
## What

Adds a per-turn execution snapshot to the `reply_payload_sending` plugin hook as a `usageState` field, so a plugin can render a per-response usage readout (model, reasoning, fast-mode, context fill, token usage, cache-hit, and the active provider's subscription/usage-limit windows) as a **pure consumer of the contract** — no side calls.

`agent-runner` records the snapshot; `dispatch` consumes it and attaches it to the hook event. New module `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-usage-state.ts`; type `PluginHookReplyUsageState` in `hook-types.ts`.

The `limits` windows are resolved by a **core-internal**, non-blocking stale-while-revalidate helper (`src/infra/provider-usage.limits.ts`) and attached to the snapshot. Because it returns only cached data and defers refresh to a macrotask, recording the snapshot never blocks reply delivery. The data reaches plugins **only as the `limits` field on the contract** — there is intentionally no new plugin-SDK accessor to widen the SDK surface. Limits are `undefined` for api-key / unmapped providers.

## Why this hook

`reply_payload_sending` is the **one reply hook that fires on every surface**, including the Codex app-server harness, which emits no `llm_output`/`agent_end` lifecycle hooks. So it's the correct, harness-agnostic place to surface per-turn usage.

## Notes

- Purely additive: `usageState` is optional on the event; existing hook consumers are unaffected.
- One cohesive change (7 files). An earlier draft split this into a hook PR + a separate plugin-SDK usage-limits accessor (#89631); that's been folded in here and #89631 is closed — limits belong on the contract, not as a plugin-pulled API.
- Developed and running live on `v2026.6.1-beta.1`; cleanly cherry-picked onto current `main` (no conflicts). CI to confirm the compile.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: marvinthebored
